photo_description: An indoor elevator lobby with three stainless-steel elevator doors set into a dark-gray wall, dark carpet flooring, recessed fluorescent ceiling lighting, an illuminated red exit sign, and a call button panel between the left and center elevators.

findings.observation: Three elevators are present side by side, providing vertical access. Each elevator has stainless-steel sliding doors with up/down indicator displays above them. A combined call button panel is mounted on the wall between the left and center elevators.
findings.estimated_measure: Elevator door openings appear approximately 90–110 cm wide each

findings.observation: The call button panel between the left and center elevators appears to be mounted at a height that may be accessible to wheelchair users; the panel face is visible with tactile-style buttons.
findings.estimated_measure: Panel center approximately 90–110 cm from floor

findings.observation: The elevator lobby corridor in front of the elevators is wide and clear of obstacles, providing ample maneuvering space for wheelchair users.
findings.estimated_measure: Lobby depth approximately 3–4 m from elevator doors to the camera position

findings.observation: The floor is covered with low-pile dark carpet. No visible lips, ridges, or transitions are apparent in the approach area, but carpet can present slightly increased rolling resistance for wheelchair users.
Recessed fluorescent/LED ceiling panels provide bright, even overhead lighting throughout the lobby. No significant glare from windows is visible; lighting appears adequate for wayfinding.
An illuminated red EXIT sign is visible in the upper right corner of the lobby, providing emergency egress indication. No Braille or tactile wayfinding signage is visible in this view.
A red fire extinguisher is mounted on the wall to the right near a door, which is a safety feature but may protrude into the path width if positioned close to the walkway.
